payload.answer: Rejected. A single conversation_summary card containing an entire 8000 word transcript is not suitable durable memory. It is overly broad, non-atomic, likely contains transient or irrelevant details, and may preserve ambiguity poorly by presenting raw conversation content as memory. No memory should be committed unless specific durable facts, preferences, decisions, or user-authorized summaries are extracted and scoped clearly.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable, atomic entity-specific fact was identified from the proposal; the proposal is an undifferentiated transcript dump.

payload.intent: Validate a proposed durable memory write containing a full transcript as one conversation_summary card.

details: No conversation_summary transcript card was committed.
No replacement durable memory cards were created.

repair_options: Extract only durable, user-relevant facts from the transcript into separate atomic memory cards.
Summarize the conversation briefly without storing the full transcript, and mark it as a non-durable session note if appropriate.
Ask the user for permission before storing sensitive, personal, or broad transcript-derived information.
Include timestamps or context only when needed to avoid presenting outdated or superseded information as current.
